summary: Re-verified Sales Lead Advance Rejection flow end-to-end after iteration 154 fixes. All four review items pass: (a) /send-to-accountant now sets current_stage_id='stg_accountant_approval' — kanban 'Accountant Approval' column populates and per-card Verify/Reject buttons render; (b) Accountant role can navigate to /crm-sales via the AppHeader top-nav 'Sales CRM' link; (c) /crm-sales loads for accountant without 403; (d) backend pytest test_lead_advance_reject_resubmit.py still passes 1/1 (~27s).

issues: Default landing view is List for accountant; Verify/Reject buttons only render in Kanban view. Accountant must manually switch to Kanban to see verify-payment-/reject-payment-{lead_id}. Consider defaulting accountant to Kanban or surfacing per-row Verify/Reject in List view too.

what_works: POST /api/crm/leads/{lead_id}/send-to-accountant now updates current_stage_id to 'stg_accountant_approval' and onboarding_status to 'accountant_pending'.
Accountant login → AppHeader shows 'Sales CRM' top-nav entry; clicking lands on /crm-sales successfully (no 403).
/crm-sales Kanban view: 'Accountant Approval' column renders with count=1 and the seeded lead card displays both data-testid 'verify-payment-{lead_id}' and 'reject-payment-{lead_id}' buttons.
Click Reject → window.prompt 'Reason for rejecting this advance? (will be sent to Sales)' → POST /api/crm/leads/{lead_id}/accountant-reject returns 200.

After reject: lead bounces back to stg_payment_collect (Deal Close column count goes 1→2 for admin, Accountant Approval drops to 0), and the red banner with data-testid 'adv-rejected-card-{lead_id}' renders on the lead card with the exact rejection reason text and the post-reject instruction.

Backend pytest /app/backend/tests/test_lead_advance_reject_resubmit.py: 1 passed in 26.91s (asserts entire reject → re-collect → re-send → verify cycle, including current_stage_id='stg_accountant_approval' after send-to-accountant).
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/src/pages/CRMSales.jsx remains at >3700 lines (>700 threshold) — still recommended to be split into smaller modules. Not blocking but ongoing tech debt.
window.prompt is still used to collect the rejection reason. Functional but non-accessible / not stylable; consider migrating to the existing Dialog/AlertDialog pattern.

Accountant landing on /accounts-board after login is fine, but the accountant has no quick visual indicator (badge/notification count) on the 'Sales CRM' top-nav link when leads are waiting in 'Accountant Approval'. Adding a count badge would improve discoverability.
test_credentials: admin@constructionos.com / Demo@1234 (super admin), accountant@constructionos.com / Demo@1234. sales@constructionos.com seed user does not exist; pytest fixture falls back to admin.
seed_data_creation: Seeded one lead 'AccountantPendingTest c04f59' (lead_5d4f43a28e62) via /app/test_reports/seed_pending_lead.py into 'accountant_pending' state for the UI Reject test. Lead was deleted from MongoDB after testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All gaps from iteration 154 are resolved. Accountant now has full Sales CRM access, the Accountant Approval column populates correctly, and per-card Verify/Reject buttons appear (in Kanban view). Backend pytest covers the entire loop. Minor UX nit: accountant must switch to Kanban view to see Verify/Reject buttons (default is List).
rca of the issue: Previous iteration confirmed the rejection banner fix at the data layer but identified two gaps: (1) send-to-accountant did not move current_stage_id, leaving 'Accountant Approval' column empty and gating Verify/Reject buttons; (2) accountant nav had no link to /crm-sales. Iteration 155 fixes verified: backend now sets current_stage_id='stg_accountant_approval' on send-to-accountant; AppHeader/Sidebar config now includes 'accountant' role for /crm-sales; CRMSales/dashboard/leads/overview endpoints accept UserRole.ACCOUNTANT. End-to-end UI + pytest validation pass.
